schemes.name: PM-KISAN (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi)
schemes.benefit_summary: Direct income support of Rs. 6000 per year, paid in three Rs. 2000 instalments to your bank account.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a small or marginal farmer holding cultivable land in your name.
Total land holding must be 2 hectares (about 5 acres) or less, as per state land records.
Must have an Aadhaar number linked to an active bank account.
Income tax payers, government employees, and pensioners drawing over Rs. 10,000 per month are excluded.
application_steps: 1. Visit the PM-KISAN portal or your nearest Common Service Centre (CSC).
2. Click 'New Farmer Registration' and enter your Aadhaar, mobile number, and state.
3. Fill land ownership details exactly as they appear on your patta or pahani.
4. Upload a scanned bank passbook page and submit.
5. Track approval status under 'Beneficiary Status' using your Aadhaar number.

schemes.name: PMFBY (Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana)
schemes.benefit_summary: Subsidised crop insurance against drought, flood, pest attack and post-harvest losses, with the farmer paying only 1.5 to 2 percent of the sum insured.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all farmers, including sharecroppers and tenant farmers, growing notified crops in notified areas.
Compulsory for farmers who have taken a Kisan Credit Card or seasonal crop loan; voluntary for others.
Must enrol before the cut-off date set for the season (Kharif or Rabi) by your state.
Land records or a tenancy certificate proving cultivation rights are required.
application_steps: 1. Visit the National Crop Insurance Portal or your nearest bank, PACS, or CSC.
2. Choose Kharif or Rabi season and select your notified crop.
3. Enter survey number, area sown, and upload land record plus bank passbook.
4. Pay the farmer share of the premium online or at the bank.
5. Save the policy receipt; report any crop loss within 72 hours via the Crop Insurance app.

schemes.name: Tamil Nadu State Crop Loan (Kisan Credit Card)
schemes.benefit_summary: Short-term crop loan up to Rs. 3 lakh at 4 percent effective interest after state and central interest subvention, via cooperative or commercial banks in Tamil Nadu.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a resident farmer of Tamil Nadu cultivating land in your name or as a registered tenant.
Loan amount is calculated from a state-fixed scale of finance based on crop and acreage.
Must have a clean credit record with the cooperative society or bank.
Loan must be repaid within the crop season to keep the 4 percent subsidised rate.
application_steps: 1. Visit your nearest Primary Agricultural Cooperative Credit Society (PACCS) or commercial bank branch.
2. Apply for a Kisan Credit Card with land patta, chitta, Aadhaar, and a passport-size photo.
3. Bank officials inspect the land and fix the loan limit per the scale of finance.
4. Sign the loan agreement and collect the KCC and passbook.
5. Withdraw against the limit each season and repay within 12 months to retain the subsidy.

schemes.name: Soil Health Card Scheme
schemes.benefit_summary: Free soil testing every two years with a printed Soil Health Card listing nutrient status and crop-wise fertiliser recommendations.
eligibility_criteria: Open to every Indian farmer, with no land size limit.
Land must be cultivable and identifiable by survey number.
Soil sample must be collected as per the prescribed grid (one sample per 2.5 hectares irrigated or 10 hectares rain-fed).
application_steps: 1. Contact your block-level Agriculture Officer or Krishi Vigyan Kendra.
2. Request soil sample collection for your survey number; an officer will visit and collect samples.
3. Samples are sent to a state soil testing lab; results are uploaded to the Soil Health Card portal.
4. Collect the printed card from the agriculture office or download it from the portal.
5. Follow the crop-wise fertiliser recommendations printed on the card for the next two seasons.
